context.description: This page discusses universal symbolism and how archetypal images preserve meaning across cultures and eras. It uses radioactivity warning signs as an example of the challenge of communicating danger over extremely long time spans. The text then connects these shared patterns to archetypes and the collective unconscious.
normalization_notes: Source language is German; summary and tags are normalized to English.
Book title inferred from file name.

rows.paragraph_text: Universal symbols retain meaning across cultures and eras, even when specific images change. The page uses radioactive waste warning signs as an example of the difficulty of preserving danger communication over very long time spans, then frames archetypes and the collective unconscious as robust symbolic structures.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion page 70 of Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2; universal symbolism, radioactivity warning signs, archetypes, collective unconscious
